events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need analyze schedule. Let's parse image. Activities with durations and dependencies. Disruption: Activity P (Flooring) needs 1 extra workday — material delivery slipped. Need project finish delay in workdays, whether completion date slips, what drives answer.

We need compute critical path and float. Let's reconstruct schedule. Early-start bars. Workdays axis. Activities:

A Mobilize & layout [2d] predecessor - (none). Starts 0? Bar from 0 to 2.
B Underground rough-in [3d] pred A. Starts 2? Bar 2-5.
C Slab pour [1d] pred B. Starts 5-6.
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C. Hatched cure lag 6-9.
D Framing [6d] pred CC. Starts 9-15.
E Roof dry-in [4d] pred D. Starts 15-19.
F Electrical rough [6d] pred E. Starts 19-25.
G Plumbing top-out [4d] pred E. Starts 19-23.
H HVAC rough [2d] pred E. Starts 19-21.
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H. Starts 25-26 (after F finishes latest 25). Red inspection hold.
J Insulation [4d] pred I. Starts 26-30.
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J. Starts 30-31.
L Drywall hang [5d] pred K. Starts 31-36.
M Tape & finish [3d] pred L. Starts 36-39.
N Paint [1d] pred M. Starts 39-40.
O Trim carpentry [3d] pred N. Starts 40-43.
P Flooring [1d] pred N. Starts 40-41.
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P. Starts 43-44.

Project finish 44 workdays.

Now disruption: P needs 1 extra workday, duration 2d, starts 40, finishes 42. Q depends on both O and P. O finishes 43 unchanged. P finishes 42, before O. So Q can still start at 43, finish 44. No project delay. Float on P? Let's compute. P is not on critical path; O is longer (3d) and drives Q. P has 2 days of float? Actually P duration 1, O duration 3. Q start = max(ES_O+3, ES_P+1). ES_P = 40. If P takes 2, finish 42; O finish 43. Q start 43. So no delay. Value 0. Completion date does not slip. Driver: P has float because trim carpentry O is the critical predecessor to final inspection Q and takes longer than P.

Need produce JSON with required fields. decision? This is schedule impact assessment, not pass/fail of work quality. The schema says decision pass|fail|needs_more_info. For schedule disruption with no delay, maybe decision pass? But pass/fail maybe for work condition. Since no dangerous/noncompliant, decision pass. risk low. work_stage in-progress. percent_complete estimate? The schedule shows many activities; observed in-progress. We can estimate overall progress maybe around 70%? But not asked. Need fill. Could set percent_complete maybe 0? But work_stage in-progress. We can estimate based on schedule: at disruption point maybe P delayed but not started? Actually stage of work when observed in-progress. We can estimate overall project percent complete by workdays completed? Project total 44 workdays, current maybe around activity P/O not started? The disruption note about P material delivery slipped suggests P is upcoming. But stage in-progress overall. Percent complete maybe 85? Let's think. The task: "decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid". We need workable list: activities that can still start. Since P delayed by 1 day, but O continues; Q delayed? Actually no. Work can proceed: O, maybe P after delay, Q. But if P delayed, O unaffected. Workable activities at this point? Need identify from schedule. The question: "Report the project finish delay in workdays in the value field (0 if the float absorbs it), state whether the completion date slips, and identify what drives your answer." Also use workable for list of activity IDs when task asks what work can still start. It asks "decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid". So include workable list.

Need determine which activities are blocked. P blocked due to material delivery (1 extra day). But can start after 1 day? It needs predecessor N complete (day 40). With material slip, maybe start delayed to day 41, finish day 43? Wait P duration 2, start 41 finish 43. O starts 40 finish 43. Q starts 43. So P still not critical…
